div1.entryFree: Less, adv. in a smaller or lower degree: l. false Sonn. 20, 5. I love not l. 102, 2; cf. H4B V, 2, 110. Tp. I, 1, 47. III, 1, 78. Gent. II, 7, 11. LLL II, 17. Wint. I, 2, 282. Cor. V, 1, 19 etc. no l.: As I, 1, 116. Wint. I, 2, 392. II, 1, 54. H5 II, 2, 92. H6B II, 3, 26. Mcb. III, 1, 136 etc. nothing l. == anything but: 'tis nothing but conceit. 'tis nothing l. R2 II, 2, 34. and yet, methinks, my father's execution was nothing l. than bloody tyranny, H6A II, 5, 100. ne'er the l. == not less on this account, or for this: I will love thee ne'er the l. Shr. I, 1, 77. l. happier == Shr. I, 1, 77 happy, R2 II, 1, 49. Peculiar use after a negative: nor a man that fears you l. than he, Cor. I, 4, 14 (instead of fears you more); cf. Wint. III, 2, 57 and Cymb. I, 4, 23.
